item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is a very good ending to a story, but I question whether it stands on its own as an article. I'm also not a big fan of the Foundation working with Anderson Robotics to make a paranormal machine, even if it might make more sense further in the future, to say nothing of "Bumaro Plaza". Sheesh.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is honestly one of my favorite SCP's on the site. It captured my interest with that amazing image you found, then it drew me in from the beginning to the end. I've never been the type for canons, but because it was can-ren-con period, I figured I'd take a gander at a few teams, and I was not disappointed in the slightest with this entry. The entire thing stood out to me, the concept was nailed, the narrative built up was great, and I simply had to give this a +1.

Nice one, Rounderhouse~

snipped

item.title: Operational Staff Post
item.content:encoded:
explode.png is by Adnan Islam, under CC BY 2.0.

reactor.png is by Alexander Blecher, blecher.info, under CC BY-SA 3.0.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
OK, so last entry of the series and from front to back, the series had been often great and entertaining. I will admit that I haven't felt too "connected" to the Maliz family, it would have been better if we had more explanations on how the feud between Nobody and White Suit affected the whole family. Plus, in the end, I feel like we haven't all the explanations about the everything, but it's probably me.

Otherwise, a +1 because it's still a really cool finale of a really cool series.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hmm… that's probably something that the original author should address, since deleting the extra "the" makes it unclear what the "they" refers to. As a general note, for cases like this, you can send a PM directly to the author to let them know that a typo was found.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Third paragraph of the description has a typo - "In addition, the they possess a deleterious effect on surveillance data depicting them."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What an incredible ride this was, I remember when I read the first submission and I knew nothing about Nobody… (there’s a sentence) … and now I feel like I’ve build up and close of an epic yarn crossing tales and SCPs with a hell of a satisfying close. BRavo to the whole team, I was happy to crit a few of these but my goodness all together they’re wonderful.

+1
